title: Risk of second primary malignancies after bladder cancer diagnosed at a young age: a SEER population-based analysis.
abstract: Bladder cancer diagnosed at a young age is uncommon, but survivors often have long life expectancy, making long-term risks such as second primary malignancies (SPMs) clinically relevant. However, the incidence and patterns of SPMs in young-onset bladder cancer survivors remain poorly characterized. We aimed to quantify the risk and distribution of SPMs in this population.
